title: JD Parsing Output Schema
description: Structured signals extracted from a Job Description for candidate ranking

required_skills.description: Canonical skill names that are absolutely required

critical_skills.description: Subset of required_skills that are hard disqualifiers if missing. For this JD: embeddings-retrieval, vector-search, python, ranking-evaluation

skill_weights.description: Custom weights per skill for deep scoring

skill_functional_equivalents.description: Maps each required skill to functionally equivalent signals that may appear in candidate profiles under different names. Critical for catching candidates who have the skill but not the keyword.

role_archetype.description: What mode this role fundamentally requires. Drives trajectory scoring — a builder role rewards candidates with shipped systems over publications.

disqualifiers.description: Hard disqualifiers. Any candidate matching one is excluded before scoring.

disqualifying_company_patterns.description: Companies whose presence as the ONLY employer type disqualifies a candidate. Conditional: if candidate has prior product-company experience, disqualifier does not apply.

company_names.description: Lowercase company name patterns to match against candidate employer history

disqualify_if.description: only-employer = disqualify only if ALL roles are at these companies. any-employer = disqualify regardless.

exception.description: Condition under which the disqualifier does not apply

title_chaser_signals.description: Operationalized definition of title-chasing for the feature extractor to detect

max_avg_tenure_months.description: If average tenure across company switches is below this, flag as title-chaser

min_company_switches.description: Minimum number of company switches required to apply the check

requires_title_escalation.description: Each switch must show a title upgrade to count as title-chasing

soft_penalties.description: Signals that reduce score but do not hard-reject. e.g. long-notice-period, framework-enthusiast, poc-only-experience

code_recency_months.description: Must have written production code in last N months

min_product_company_years.description: Minimum years at product companies (not services/consulting)

behavioral_availability_signals.description: Signals from platform behavior that indicate whether a candidate is genuinely available. Explicitly called out in this JD — a perfect-on-paper candidate who is inactive is not actually available.

penalize_inactive.description: Whether to down-weight candidates with low platform activity

inactive_threshold_days.description: Candidates with no activity beyond this many days get penalized

response_rate_threshold.description: Candidates below this recruiter response rate get penalized

availability_weight_in_score.description: How much behavioral availability affects the final composite score

implied_signals.description: Signals inferred from JD language not explicit in skill lists. e.g. systems-thinker, product-mindset, pre-llm-era-ml-experience

text_embedding.description: 384-dim MiniLM embedding of full JD text. Null until rank.py runs.

extraction_confidence.description: If overall_confidence < 0.7, scorer falls back to pure semantic similarity and disables rule-based disqualifiers.
